summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, AI / computing, Democracy / anti-fascism. The strongest items in this run are Trump news at a glance: Melania Trump’s weird disavowal of Epstein is getting weirder, ‘We can’t lose’: all eyes on potential 2028 Democratic contenders at big party gathering for this year’s midterms, and Florida launches investigation into OpenAI.

mainItems.title: Florida launches investigation into OpenAI
mainItems.summary: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier is launching an investigation into OpenAI over public safety and national security risks, as reported earlier by Reuters.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.summary: Suspects allegedly bought 14 hospice companies and used stolen identities to fraudulently bill state health plan Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox California authorities have filed felony charges…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Netflix’s TV games get a big boost with Jackbox collection
mainItems.summary: Netflix's party-focused lineup of TV games just got three excellent new additions: Jackbox's Drawful 2, Fibbage 4, and Quiplash 3, available as the Jackbox Party Essentials pack on the platform.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Malcolm in the Middle: Life’s Still Unfair review – the TV magic they’ve created here is absolutely miraculous
mainItems.summary: This revival does the impossible: it’s effortlessly funny and refreshing, and Bryan Cranston’s performance is unmissable. They have to make more At this point, Bryan Cranston is firmly entrenched as one of the world’s finest actors.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: The EFF is quitting X
mainItems.summary: The digital privacy non-profit Electronic Frontier Foundation will no longer be posting on X as of Thursday, largely due to a sharp decline in views on the platform over the past several years.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: Financial Literacy Courses are Expanding in Connecticut, Thanks to New Requirement
mainItems.summary: When Stamford High School students arrive at their personal finance class, they’re greeted by a stock ticker and a TV monitor showing the day’s business and financial news.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: Over the Hill looks like SnowRunner's cosy off-roading cousin, and its new trailer shows off muddy motoring in Algeria ahead of release later this year
mainItems.summary: I've really dug Funselektor's minimalistic and relaxed vibe-heavy arcade racers over the past few years. Absolute Drift 's brill, Art Of Rally 's great, and I also liked retro F1 management sim Golden Lap .
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Read an Excerpt From Make Me Better by Sarah Gailey
mainItems.summary: An eerily seductive look at the desire for community connection and self-improvement—and the darkest places inside us all… The post Read an Excerpt From Make Me Better by Sarah Gailey appeared first on Reactor .
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

mainItems.title: This doctor turned a 31-foot RV into one of the country's only mobile OB-GYN clinics
mainItems.summary: Mary Fariba Afsari's book, Labor, is a portrait of reproductive healthcare in post- Dobbs America. Her book also is about her Iranian heritage and her grandmother's death from an illegal abortion.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: World’s Largest Group of Chimps Waging Deadly ‘Civil War,’ Scientists Discover
mainItems.summary: At least 24 chimpanzees have been killed in a war that has split the Ngogo group of wild chimpanzees in two, turning former kin into enemies.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: How can I get a more bubbly, open crumb in my focaccia?: Two key focaccia tips from our recent podcast episode.
mainItems.summary: In every episode of our podcast Things Bakers Know , we answer baking questions from our listeners.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Lower-stakes, but high fit for the maker/craft side of your brain.

trailers.title: Legends | Official Trailer | Netflix
trailers.summary: To win the war on drugs, a group of everyday customs officers, untrained in espionage, were sent undercover into some of Britain’s most dangerous gangs. Inspired by an incredible true story.
trailers.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: 'Not our war' say Christians under siege this Easter, as Israel invades south Lebanon
